user_prompt: first, look at `inc\version.h`, implement the strings in `src\version.c` using as much of compiler preprocessor definitions as possible.
`syncToCompileTime` needs to use `compiler_time_str`, if `compiler_time_str` is in the future, sync the physical RTC to it.
somehow make sure `version.c` is compiled every build, maybe something to do with `platformio.ini`, maybe adding a script to `tools`
it is acceptable if `src\version.c` is automatically generated by a tool, as long as `inc\version.h` stays consistent
do not touch other files not mentioned
agent_reply: - Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.
Verification: `pio run` passed. I also ran a second build and confirmed PlatformIO recompiled `src/version.c.o` again, so the pre-build regeneration is doing its job.
